arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ عَنِ ابْنِ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ، جُرْمَوْقَيْنِ مِنْ لُبُودٍ يَمْسَحُ عَلَيْهِمَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Idrīs → Yazīd ibn Abī Ziyād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, from Ibn Idrīs, from Yazīd ibn Abī Ziyād, who said: "I saw Ibrāhīm wearing two overshoes (Jurmuq) made of felt, and he was wiping over them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ عَنْ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الْجُنُبِ يَعْرَقُ فِي الثَّوْبِ حَتَّى يَنْعَصِرَ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي فِيهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mubārak → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, from Mubārak, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a person in a state of major ritual impurity (Junub) who sweats in a garment until it can be wrung out. He said: "He prays in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا هِشَامٌ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ وَالْحَائِضِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Hishām → ‘Ikrimah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Hushaym told us, saying: Hishām informed us, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, that he saw no harm in the sweat of the Junub and the menstruating woman.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا يُونُسُ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ وَالْحَائِضِ

english.narrator: Hishām → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hishām told us, saying: Yūnus informed us, from Al-Ḥasan, that he saw no harm in the sweat of the Junub and the menstruating woman.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ خُثَيْمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، فِي الْجُنُبِ يَعْرَقُ فِي الثَّوْبِ، فَيَأْخُذُ عَرَقُهُ، فَيَتَمَسَّحُ بِهِ: لَمْ يَرَ بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Ath-Thaqafī → ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Uthmān ibn Khuthaym → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Ath-Thaqafī told us, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Uthmān ibn Khuthaym, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, regarding the Junub who sweats in a garment and takes his sweat and wipes himself with it: "He saw no harm in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ وَالْحَائِضِ

english.narrator: Ibn Mubārak → Hishām → ‘Ikrimah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn Mubārak told us, from Hishām, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, that he saw no harm in the sweat of the Junub and the menstruating woman.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّهَا كَانَتْ لَا تَرَى بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Al-Qāsim → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah told us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from Al-Qāsim, from ‘Ā’ishah, that she saw no harm in the sweat of the Junub.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سُلَيْمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ لَا يَرَى بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ بَأْسًا فِي الثَّوْبِ، وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ نَجَاسَةٌ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Sulaym → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Sulaym told us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: "He saw no harm in the sweat of the Junub on the garment, and there is no impurity upon him in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ، عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ حَمَّادًا، عَنْ الْحَائِضِ تَعْرَقُ فِي ثِيَابِهَا، قَالَ: إِنَّمَا تَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ الْمَجُوسُ

english.narrator: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd → Al-‘Alā’ → Ḥammād
english.text: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd told us, from Al-‘Alā’, who said: I asked Ḥammād about the menstruating woman sweating in her clothes. He said: "Only the Zoroastrians (Majūs) do that [consider it impure]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَعْرَقُ فِي الثَّوْبِ وَهُوَ جُنُبٌ، ثُمَّ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → Mālik ibn Anas → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn Mahdī told us, from Mālik ibn Anas, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to sweat in a garment while he was Junub, then he would pray in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ وَرْدَانَ، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ فِي ثِيَابِهِ

english.narrator: Ḥātim ibn Wardān → Burd → Makḥūl
english.text: Ḥātim ibn Wardān told us, from Burd, from Makḥūl, that he saw no harm in the sweat of the Junub in his clothes.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِعَرَقِ الْجُنُبِ فِي الثَّوْبِ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → ‘Aṭā’ ibn as-Sā’ib → Ash-Sha‘bī
english.text: Ibn Mahdī told us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from ‘Aṭā’ ibn as-Sā’ib, from Ash-Sha‘bī, who said: "There is no harm in the sweat of the Junub on the garment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْجُنُبِ يَعْرَقُ فِي الثَّوْبِ، قَالَ: لَا يَضُرُّهُ، وَلَا يَنْضَحَهُ بِالْمَاءِ

english.narrator: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Abū Ḥamzah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ told us, from Abū Ḥamzah, from Ibrāhīm, regarding the Junub sweating in the garment. He said: "It does not harm him, nor should he sprinkle it with water."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ زُبَيْدٍ، وَالْأَعْمَشِ، قَالَا: كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ، يَنْتَهِي إِلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ فِي نَعْلَيْهِ، أَوْ فِي خُفَّيْهِ السِّرْقَيْنُ فَيَمْسَحُهُمَا، ثُمَّ يَدْخُلُ فَيُصَلِّي

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → Layth → Zubayd + Al-A‘mash → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Jarīr told us, from Layth, from Zubayd and Al-A‘mash, who said: "Ibrāhīm used to arrive at the door of the mosque with manure on his sandals or leather socks, so he would wipe them, then enter and pray."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ الْمُنْذِرِ، سَأَلَ عُرْوَةَ بْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنِ الرَّوْثِ يُصِيبُ النَّعْلَ، قَالَ: امْسَحْهُ وَصَلِّ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → ‘Āṣim ibn al-Mundhir → ‘Urwah ibn az-Zubayr
english.text: Ibn Mahdī told us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from ‘Āṣim ibn al-Mundhir, who asked ‘Urwah ibn az-Zubayr about dung getting on the sandal. He said: "Wipe it off and pray in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ ثَابِتِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُهُ يَحِكُّ نَعْلَهُ، أَوْ خُفَّهُ عَلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ قَالَ: يَذْكُرُ أَنَّهُ طَهُورٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mis‘ar → Thābit ibn ‘Ubayd
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Mis‘ar, from Thābit ibn ‘Ubayd, who said: "I saw him rubbing his sandal or leather sock at the door of the mosque." He said: "He mentioned that it is a purification."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَشْتَدُّونَ فِي الرَّوْثِ الرَّطْبِ إِذَا كَانَ فِي الْخُفِّ

english.narrator: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd → Mughīrah → Ḥammād
english.text: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd told us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, who said: "They used to be strict regarding wet dung if it was on the leather sock."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ قَالَ: كَانَ عَزِيزًا عَلَى طَاوُسٍ، إِذَا دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ أَنْ لَا يَقْلِبَ خُفَّهُ، أَوْ نَعْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Mis‘ar → ‘Abd al-Karīm → Ṭāwūs
english.text: Abū Usāmah told us, from Mis‘ar, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, who said: "It was rare for Ṭāwūs, when entering the mosque, not to flip his leather sock or sandal [to check/clean it]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أنا حَجَّاجٌ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، أَنَّهُمَا لَمْ يَرَيَا بِدَمِ الْبَرَاغِيثِ وَالْبَعُوضِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Hushaym → Ḥajjāj → Abū Ja‘far + ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Hushaym told us, saying: Ḥajjāj informed us, from Abū Ja‘far and ‘Aṭā’, that they saw no harm in the blood of fleas and mosquitoes.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ، قَالَ: أَنَا أَشْعَثُ بْنُ سَوَّارٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: كَانَ الْحَسَنُ، لَا يَرَى بِدَمِ الذُّبَابِ وَالْبَعُوضِ وَالْبَرَاغِيثِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Hishām → Ash‘ath ibn Sawwār → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hishām told us, saying: Ash‘ath ibn Sawwār informed us, from Al-Ḥasan, that he said: "Al-Ḥasan used to see no harm in the blood of flies, mosquitoes, and fleas."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ وَفِي ثَوْبِي دَمُ ذُبَابٍ، فَقُلْتُ لِأَبِي: فَقَالَ: لَا يَضُرُّكَ

english.narrator: Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → His Father (‘Urwah)
english.text: Abū Mu‘āwiyah told us, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, who said: I prayed while there was fly blood on my garment. I mentioned it to my father, and he said: "It does not harm you."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ زُهَيْرٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، قَالَا: لَا بَأْسَ بِدَمِ الْبَرَاغِيثِ

english.narrator: Al-Faḍl ibn Dukayn → Zuhayr → Jābir → ‘Āmir + ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Al-Faḍl ibn Dukayn told us, from Zuhayr, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir and ‘Aṭā’, who said: "There is no harm in the blood of fleas."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَاجِرُ بْنُ الصَّلْتِ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: انْطَلَقْتُ إِلَى مَنْزِلِ الْحَسَنِ، فَجَاءَ رَجُلٌ فَسَأَلَهُ، فَقَالَ: يَا أَبَا سَعِيدٍ، الرَّجُلُ يَبِيتُ فِي الثَّوْبِ، فَيَصْبِحُ وَفِيهِ مِنْ دَمِ الْبَرَاغِيثِ شَيْءٌ كَثِيرٌ يَغْسِلَهُ، أَوْ يَنْضَحَهُ، أَوْ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ؟ قَالَ: لَا يَنْضَحَهُ، وَلَا يَغْسِلَهُ، يُصَلِّي فِيهِ

english.narrator: Zājir ibn aṣ-Ṣalt → Al-Ḥārith ibn Mālik → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Zājir ibn aṣ-Ṣalt told us, from Al-Ḥārith ibn Mālik, who said: I went to the house of Al-Ḥasan. A man came and asked him, saying: "O Abū Sa‘īd, a man sleeps in a garment and wakes up with a lot of flea blood on it. Should he wash it, sprinkle water on it, or pray in it?" He said: "He should not sprinkle it nor wash it; he prays in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أنا هِشَامٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِدَمِ السَّمَكِ، إِلَّا أَنْ يَقْذَرَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hushaym told us, saying: Hishām informed us, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: "There is no harm in fish blood, unless it is considered filthy (repulsive)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الْقَطَّانِ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: اغْسِلْ مَا أَصَابَكَ مِنْ دَمِ الصَّيْدِ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd al-Qaṭṭān → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd al-Qaṭṭān told us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: "Wash whatever game blood gets on you."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، قَالَ: أنا أَشْعَثُ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي مُتَيَمِّمٍ مَرَّ بِمَاءٍ غَيْرَ مُحْتَاجٍ إِلَى الْوُضُوءِ فَجَاوَزَهُ، فَحَضَرْتِ الصَّلَاةُ وَلَيْسَ مَعَهُ مَاءٌ، قَالَ: يُعِيدُ التَّيَمُّمَ، لِأَنَّ قُدْرَتَهُ عَلَى الْمَاءِ تُنْقِضُ تَيَمُّمَهُ الْأَوَّلَ

english.narrator: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh told us, saying: Ash‘ath informed us, from Al-Ḥasan, that he said regarding a person who performed Tayammum and passed by water while not needing ablution (at that moment), so he passed it. Then the prayer time arrived and he had no water. He said: "He repeats the Tayammum, because his ability to access water invalidates his first Tayammum."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: الْقَيْءُ وَالْخَمْرُ وَالدَّمُ بِمَنْزِلَةٍ يَعْنِي: فِي الثَّوْبِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī told us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: "Vomit, wine (Khamr), and blood are of the same status," meaning: regarding the garment (in terms of impurity).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمُحَارِبِيُّ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَصَابَ ثَوْبَكَ خَمْرٌ فَاغْسِلَهُ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنِ الدَّمِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Muḥammad al-Muḥāribī → Layth → Mujāhid
english.text: ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Muḥammad al-Muḥāribī told us, from Layth, from Mujāhid, who said: "If wine gets on your garment, wash it; it is more severe than blood."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعَاذٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَرُشَّ الْجُنُبُ وَالْحَائِضُ الْمَسْجِدَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘ādh → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan + Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Mu‘ādh told us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan and Ibn Sīrīn, that they said: "There is no harm if the Junub and the menstruating woman sprinkle (clean/water) the mosque."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، أَنَّ أَعْرَابِيًّا بَالَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، فَدَعَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِذَنُوبٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ، فَصَبَّهُ عَلَى بَوْلِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Anas
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Yazīd ibn Hārūn told us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from Anas, that a Bedouin urinated in the mosque, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called for a large bucket of water and poured it over his urine.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنْ قَيْسٍ قَالَ: بَالَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، فَأَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَصُبَّ عَلَى بَوْلِهِ مَاءٌ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Ismā‘īl → Qays
english.text: Abū Usāmah told us, saying: Ismā‘īl told us, from Qays, who said: "A Bedouin urinated in the mosque, so the Prophet ﷺ ordered that water be poured over his urine."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: دَخَلَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ الْمَسْجِدَ وَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِيهِ فَبَالَ، فَأَمَرَ بِسِجِلٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ، فَأَفْرَغَ عَلَى بَوْلِهِ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Mushir → Muḥammad ibn ‘Amr → Abū Salamah → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Mushir told us, from Muḥammad ibn ‘Amr, from Abū Salamah, from Abū Hurayrah, who said: "A Bedouin entered the mosque while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was in it and urinated. He ordered a bucket of water and poured it over his urine."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا يَحْيَى بْنُ عِيسَى الرَّمْلِيُّ، عَنْ رَزِينٍ قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، فَقَالَ لَهُ: إِنِّي أَخْرُجُ فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْمَطِيرَةِ فَأَدُوسُ الطِّينَ، قَالَ: صَلِّ، قَالَ: إِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يَكُونَ فِيهَا النَّتَنُ وَالْقَذَرَةُ، فَكَأَنَّهُ غَضَبَ، فَقَالَ: أَنْ كُنْتُ تَدُوسُ الطِّينَ بِرِجْلَيْكَ فَخُذْ مَعَكَ مَاءً فَاغْسِلْ بِهِ رِجْلَيْكَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn ‘Īsā ar-Ramlī → Razīn → Abū Ja‘far
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn ‘Īsā ar-Ramlī told us, from Razīn, who said: A man came to Abū Ja‘far and said to him: "I go out on a rainy night and step on mud." He said: "Pray." The man said: "I fear that there might be stench and filth in it." He seemed to get angry and said: "If you step on the mud with your feet, then take water with you and wash your feet with it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ: إِلَّا مَسَحْتُهُمَا وَدَخَلَتُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Hishām → Qatādah → Ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Hishām, from Qatādah, from Ibn al-Musayyib, that he said to a man (regarding shoes/mud): "Why didn't you just wipe them and enter?"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ، يَخُوضُ طِينَ الْمَطَرِ، وَيَدْخُلُ الْمَسْجِدَ فَيُصَلِّي وَلَا يَتَوَضَّأُ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Ḥajjāj → Al-Ḥakam → ‘Alī
english.text: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth told us, from Ḥajjāj, from Al-Ḥakam, who said: "‘Alī used to wade through rain mud, enter the mosque, and pray without performing ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ حَكِيمِ بْنِ الدَّيْلَمِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ مُغَفَّلٍ، فِي يَوْمِ مَطَرٍ، قَائِمًا يُصَلِّي إِلَى سَارِيَةٍ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، وَعَلَى رِجْلَيْهِ مِثْلُ الْخَلْخَالَيْنِ أَوِ الْحِجَالَيْنِ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Ḥakīm ibn ad-Daylam → Ibn Mughaffal
english.text: Sharīk told us, from Ḥakīm ibn ad-Daylam, who said: "I saw Ibn Mughaffal on a rainy day standing and praying towards a pillar in the mosque, and on his legs was [mud] like two anklets or two shackles."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَلْقَمَةَ، وَالْأَسْوَدَ، يَخُوضَانِ مَاءَ الْمَطَرِ، وَأَنَّ الْمَيَازِيبَ تَنْشَعِبُ، ثُمَّ دَخَلَا الْمَسْجِدَ، فَصَلَّيَا وَلَمْ يَتَوَضَّآ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Jābir → ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn al-Aswad → ‘Alqamah + Al-Aswad
english.text: Sharīk told us, from Jābir, from ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn al-Aswad, who said: "I saw ‘Alqamah and Al-Aswad wading through rain water while the gutters were pouring, then they entered the mosque and prayed without performing ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ إِذَا دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ فِي الْأَمْطَارِ نَظَرَ إِلَى خُفَّيْهِ، فَإِنْ كَانَ فِيهِمَا طِينٌ قَلِيلٌ مَسَحَهُ، ثُمَّ دَخَلَ فَصَلَّى، وَإِنْ كَانَ كَثِيرًا خَلَعَهُمَا وَأَمَرَ بِهِمَا فَغَسَلَا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hushaym told us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: "When he entered the mosque during rains, he would look at his leather socks. If there was a little mud on them, he would wipe it off, then enter and pray. If it was a lot, he would take them off and order for them to be washed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُنَا يَخُوضُونَ الْمَاءَ وَالطِّينَ إِلَى مَسَاجِدَهُمْ، وَيصَلُّونَ وَلَا يَغْسُلونَ أَرْجَلَهُمْ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Hushaym told us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "Our companions used to wade through water and mud to their mosques, and pray without washing their feet."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ الْمَخْتَارِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْقَاسِمَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ، دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ يَوْمَ مَطَرٍ وَلَمْ يَغْسِلْ رِجْلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā → Al-Mukhtār ibn Sa‘d → Al-Qāsim ibn Muḥammad
english.text: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā told us, from Al-Mukhtār ibn Sa‘d, who said: "I saw Al-Qāsim ibn Muḥammad enter the mosque on a rainy day and he did not wash his feet."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَخَوضُ الْمَطَرَ، فَسَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، فَقَالَ: صَلِّهِ قَالَ: وَسَمِعْتُ أَبَا إِسْحَاقَ، يَقُولُ: كَانُوا يَخُوضُونَ، ثُمَّ يُصَلُّونَ وَلَا يَحْمِلُونَ مَعَهُمُ الْأَكْوَازَ

english.narrator: Abū Dāwūd → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam | Abū Isḥāq
english.text: Abū Dāwūd told us, from Shu‘bah, who said: I was wading through rain, so I asked Al-Ḥakam, and he said: "Pray in it." He said: And I heard Abū Isḥāq saying: "They used to wade [through mud], then pray, and they did not carry water jugs with them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ الْمُهَاجِرِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ، يَخُوضُ الرِّدَاغَ فِي خُفَّيْهِ، ثُمَّ يُصَلِّي فِيهِمَا

english.narrator: Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī → Zā’idah → Ibrāhīm ibn al-Muhājir → ‘Amr ibn ‘Abd Allāh → ‘Abd Allāh (Ibn Mas‘ūd)
english.text: Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī told us, from Zā’idah, from Ibrāhīm ibn al-Muhājir, from ‘Amr ibn ‘Abd Allāh, who said: "‘Abd Allāh used to wade through mud in his leather socks, then pray in them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى، قَالَ: "مَرَرْتُ مَعَ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ فِي طَرِيقٍ، فقَطَرَ عَلَيْهِ مِيزَابٌ، فَسَأَلَ عَنْهُ، فَقِيلَ: إِنَّهُ نَظِيفٌ، فَلَمْ يَلْتَفِتْ إِلَيْهِ، وَلَمْ يُبَالِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī → Abū Mūsā → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī told us, from Abū Mūsā, who said: "I passed with Ibn Sīrīn on a path, and a gutter dripped on him. He asked about it, and it was said: 'It is clean.' So he did not pay attention to it and did not care."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَسْعَدَةَ، قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ الرُّومِيُّ قَالَ: كَانَ عُثْمَانُ، يَقُومُ مِنِ اللَّيْلِ فَيَلِي طَهُورَهُ بِنَفْسِهِ، فَيُقَالُ لَهُ: لَوْ أَمَرْتَ بَعْضَ الْخَدَمِ، فَقَالَ: إِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَلِيَهُ بِنَفْسِي

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → ‘Alī ibn Mas‘adah → ‘Abd Allāh ar-Rūmī → ‘Uthmān
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Abū Usāmah told us, from ‘Alī ibn Mas‘adah, who said: ‘Abd Allāh ar-Rūmī told us, saying: ‘Uthmān used to stand up at night and attend to his own purification water himself. It was said to him: "If you ordered some of the servants..." He said: "I love to attend to it myself."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْمَدَنِيِّ قَالَ: خَصْلَتَانِ لَمْ يَكُنْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَكِلْهُمَا إِلَى أَحَدٍ مِنْ أَهْلِهِ، كَانَ يُنَاوِلُ الْمِسْكِينَ بِيَدِهِ، وَيَضَعُ الطَّهُورَ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ وَيُخَمِّرُهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mūsā ibn ‘Ubaydah → Al-‘Abbās ibn ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān al-Madanī → Messenger of Allah ﷺ
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Mūsā ibn ‘Ubaydah, from Al-‘Abbās ibn ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān al-Madanī, who said: "There were two habits that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would not entrust to anyone from his family: He used to hand [charity] to the poor person with his own hand, and he would set out his purification water at night and cover it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ زَكَرِيَّا، عَنْ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ شَيْبَةَ، عَنْ طَلْقٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "عَشْرٌ مِنَ الْفِطْرَةِ: قَصُّ الشَّارِبِ، وَإِعْفَاءُ اللِّحْيَةِ، وَالسِّوَاكُ، وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقُ بِالْمَاءِ، وَقَصُّ الْأَظْفَارِ، وَغَسْلُ الْبَرَاجمِ، وَنَتْفُ الْإِبِطِ، وَحَلْقُ الْعَانَةِ، وَانْتقَاصُ الْمَاءِ" قَالَ مُصْعَبٌ: وَنَسِيتُ الْعَاشِرَةَ، إِلَّا أَنْ تَكونَ الْمَضْمَضَةَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Zakariyyā → Muṣ‘ab ibn Shaybah → Ṭalq → Ibn az-Zubayr → ‘Ā’ishah → Messenger of Allah ﷺ
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Zakariyyā, from Muṣ‘ab ibn Shaybah, from Ṭalq, from Ibn az-Zubayr, from ‘Ā’ishah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Ten things are part of the Fiṭrah (natural disposition): Trimming the mustache, letting the beard grow, using the Siwāk, sniffing water into the nose, cutting the nails, washing the knuckles, plucking the armpit hair, shaving the pubic hair, and using water for cleaning oneself (Intiqāṣ al-mā’).” Muṣ‘ab said: "And I forgot the tenth, unless it is rinsing the mouth."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِي صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: "خمْسٌ مِنَ الْفِطْرَةِ: الْخِتَانُ، وَالِاسْتِحْدَادُ، وَتَقْلِيمُ الْأَظْفَارِ، وَنَتْفُ الْإِبِطِ، وَقَصُّ الشَّارِبِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Az-Zuhrī → Sa‘īd → Abū Hurayrah → Prophet ﷺ
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah told us, from Az-Zuhrī, from Sa‘īd, from Abū Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Five things are part of the Fiṭrah: Circumcision, shaving the pubic hair, cutting the nails, plucking the armpit hair, and trimming the mustache."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قَبِيصَةُ بْنُ عُقْبَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ يَاسِرٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: الْفِطْرَةُ الْمَضْمَضَةُ، وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقُ، وَالسِّوَاكُ، وَقَصُّ الشَّارِبِ، وَنَتْفُ الْإِبِطِ، وَغَسْلُ الْبَرَاجِمِ، وَتَقْلِيمُ الْأَظْفَارِ، وَالِانْتِضَاحُ بِالْمَاءِ، وَالْخِتَانُ

english.narrator: Qabīṣah ibn ‘Uqbah → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → ‘Alī ibn Zayd → Salamah ibn Muḥammad → ‘Ammār ibn Yāsir → Messenger of Allah ﷺ
english.text: Qabīṣah ibn ‘Uqbah told us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from ‘Alī ibn Zayd, from Salamah ibn Muḥammad, from ‘Ammār ibn Yāsir, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The Fiṭrah consists of rinsing the mouth, sniffing water into the nose, using the Siwāk, trimming the mustache, plucking the armpit hair, washing the knuckles, cutting the nails, sprinkling water [on the private parts after cleansing], and circumcision."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: "سِتٌّ مِنْ فِطْرَةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ: قَصُّ الشَّارِبِ، وَالسِّوَاكُ، وَالْفَرْقُ، وَقَصُّ الْأَظْفَارِ، وَالِاسْتِنْجَاءُ، وَحَلْقُ الْعَانَةِ" قَالَ: ثَلَاثَةٌ فِي الرَّأْسِ، وَثَلَاثَةٌ فِي الْجَسَدِ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Layth → Mujāhid
english.text: Sharīk told us, from Layth, from Mujāhid, who said: "Six things are from the Fiṭrah of Abraham, peace be upon him: Trimming the mustache, using the Siwāk, parting the hair, cutting the nails, washing after relieving oneself (Istinjā’), and shaving the pubic hair." He said: "Three in the head and three in the body."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ الْعَبْديُّ، وَأَبُو أُسَامَةَ، قَالَا: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ أَيُّوبَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِنَّ لِلشَّيْطَانِ زُفَّةً يَعْنِي: بِلَّةَ طَرْفِ الْإِحْلِيلِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Muḥammad ibn Bishr al-‘Abdī + Abū Usāmah → Mis‘ar → ‘Amr ibn Ayyūb → Ash-Sha‘bī
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Bishr al-‘Abdī and Abū Usāmah told us, saying: Mis‘ar told us, from ‘Amr ibn Ayyūb, from Ash-Sha‘bī, who said: "Indeed, Satan has a puff/instigation," meaning the wetness at the tip of the urethra (causing doubt).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: مَا تَفَقَّدهُ إِنْسَانٌ إِلَّا رَأَى مَا يَكْرَهُ أَوْ يَسُوءُهُ يَعْنِي: بِلَّةَ طَرْفِ الْإِحْلِيلِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bishr → Mis‘ar → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bishr told us, saying: Mis‘ar told us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "No person inspects it but that he sees what he dislikes or what upsets him," meaning the wetness at the tip of the urethra.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، قَالَ: إِنَّهُ يُبِلُّ طَرْفَ الْإِحْلِيلِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bishr → Mis‘ar → Manṣūr
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bishr told us, saying: Mis‘ar told us, from Manṣūr, who said: "He wets the tip of the urethra."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ، عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ بْنِ سَهْلٍ، قَالَ: كَانُوا لَا يَتَفَقَّدُونَ ذَلِكَ التَّفقُّدَ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bishr → Mis‘ar → A man from Quraysh → Abū Umāmah ibn Sahl
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bishr told us, saying: Mis‘ar told us, from a man from Quraysh, from Abū Umāmah ibn Sahl, who said: "They did not used to inspect [it] with such inspection."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: نا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، قَالَ: مَا وَسَاوِسَهُ بِأَوْلَعَ مِمَّنْ يَرَاهَا يَعْمَلُ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bishr → Mis‘ar → ‘Amr ibn Murrah
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bishr told us, saying: Mis‘ar told us, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, who said: "His whisperings (Waswasah) are not more keen on anyone than on the one who sees them affecting him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي ذُؤيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ بْنِ سَهْلٍ، قَالَ: مَا تَفَقَّدَ رَجُلٌ ذَكَرَهُ ذَلِكَ التَّفقُّدَ إِلَّا رَأَى مَا يَكْرَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Mis‘ar → Abū Dhu’ayb → Abū Umāmah ibn Sahl
english.text: Abū Usāmah told us, from Mis‘ar, from Abū Dhu’ayb, from Abū Umāmah ibn Sahl, who said: "No man inspects his penis with such inspection except that he sees what he dislikes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ مُفَضَّلِ بْنِ مُهَلْهَلٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ تَمِيمِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ: إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ يَأْتِي الْإِنْسَانَ مِنْ قِبَلِ الْوُضُوءِ وَالشَّعْرِ وَالظُّفْرِ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Mufaḍḍal ibn Muhalhal → Manṣūr → Tamīm ibn Salamah → Ibn az-Zubayr
english.text: Abū Usāmah told us, from Mufaḍḍal ibn Muhalhal, from Manṣūr, from Tamīm ibn Salamah, who said: Ibn az-Zubayr said: "Indeed, Satan comes to the human being through ablution, hair, and nails."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: نَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِيمَنْ نَسِيَ الْمَضْمَضَةَ فِي الْوُضُوءِ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقِ، قَالَ: يُمَضْمِضُ وَيَسْتَنْشِقُ وَيُعِيدُ الصَّلَاةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → Qays ibn Sa‘d → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Yazīd ibn Hārūn told us, saying: Ḥammād ibn Salamah told us, from Qays ibn Sa‘d, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding someone who forgot rinsing the mouth and sniffing water in ablution. He said: "He rinses his mouth and sniffs water, and repeats the prayer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ بِنْتِ عَجْرَدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّى الرَّجُلُ فَنَسِيَ أَنْ يُمَضْمِضَ وَيَسْتَنْشِقَ مِنْ جَنَابَةٍ، أَعَادَ الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Ḥajjāj → ‘Ā’ishah bint ‘Ajrad → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth told us, from Ḥajjāj, from ‘Ā’ishah bint ‘Ajrad, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: "If a man prays and forgets to rinse his mouth and sniff water from Janābah (ritual bath), he repeats the rinsing of the mouth and sniffing of water."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مُثنَّى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِيمَنْ نَسِيَ الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ حَتَّى صَلَّى، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ إِعَادَةٌ

english.narrator: Ibn Mubārak → Muthannā → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Ibn Mubārak told us, from Muthannā, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding someone who forgot rinsing the mouth and sniffing water until he prayed. He said: "There is no repetition upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَنْسَى الْمَضْمَضَةَ، قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ دَخَلَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَلْيُمْضِ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ دَخَلَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَلْيُمَضْمِضْ وَيَسْتَنْشِقْ

english.narrator: Ibn Idrīs → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Ibn Idrīs told us, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who forgets rinsing the mouth. He said: "If he has entered prayer, let him proceed; and if he has not entered prayer, let him rinse his mouth and sniff water."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: يُعِيدُ الرَّجُلُ الصَّلَاةَ مِنْ نِسْيَانِ الْمَضْمَضَةِ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقِ

english.narrator: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → ‘Umar ibn ‘Āmir → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām told us, from ‘Umar ibn ‘Āmir, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "A man repeats the prayer due to forgetting rinsing the mouth and sniffing water."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، وَحَمَّادًا، وَقَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَنْسَى الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ حَتَّى يَقُومَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، قَالَ الْحَكَمُ وَقَتَادَةُ: يَمْضِي، وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ: يَنْصَرِفُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam + Ḥammād + Qatādah
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Shu‘bah, who said: I asked Al-Ḥakam, Ḥammād, and Qatādah about a man who forgets rinsing the mouth and sniffing water until he stands for prayer. Al-Ḥakam and Qatādah said: "He proceeds." Ḥammād said: "He leaves [prayer to do it]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا نَسِيَ الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ فِي الْجَنَابَةِ أَعَادَ، وَإِذَا نَسِيَ فِي الْوُضُوءِ أَجْزَأَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl → Ash-Sha‘bī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ismā‘īl, from Ash-Sha‘bī, who said: "If he forgets rinsing the mouth and sniffing water in Janābah (ritual bath), he repeats; and if he forgets in Wudu, it suffices him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ نَسِيَ الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ حَتَّى صَلَّى، قَالَ: لَا يُعِيدُ بِذَلِكَ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who forgot rinsing the mouth and sniffing water until he prayed. He said: "He does not repeat for that."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، وَأَبِي الْهَيْثَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: لَيْسَ الِاسْتِنْشَاقُ بِوَاجِبٍ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Mughīrah + Abū al-Haytham → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Sharīk told us, from Mughīrah and Abū al-Haytham, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "Sniffing water (Istinshāq) is not obligatory."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ قَالَ: إِذَا نَسِيَ الرَّجُلُ الْمَضْمَضَةَ وَالِاسْتِنْشَاقَ فَلَا يُعِيدُ

english.narrator: Asbāṭ ibn Muḥammad → Mughīrah → Ḥammād
english.text: Asbāṭ ibn Muḥammad told us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, who said: "If a man forgets rinsing the mouth and sniffing water, he does not repeat."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ: الرَّجُلُ يَنْسَى الِاسْتِنْشَاقَ، فَيَذْكُرُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ أَنَّهُ نَسِيَ، قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: يَمْضِي فِي صَلَاتِهِ قَالَ: وَقَالَ مَنْصُورٌ: وَالْمَضْمَضَةُ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Ḥasan ibn ‘Alī → Zā’idah → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Ḥasan ibn ‘Alī told us, from Zā’idah, from Manṣūr, who said: I said to Ibrāhīm: "A man forgets sniffing water, then remembers in prayer that he forgot." Ibrāhīm said: "He proceeds with his prayer." He said: And Manṣūr said: "And rinsing the mouth is like that."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي هَاشِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ بَعْضُ أُمَّهَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَتَقْرُصُ الدَّمَ عَنْ ثَوْبِهَا بِرِيقِهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Abū Hāshim → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Abū Hāshim, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: "Some of the Mothers of the Believers used to pinch off blood from their garment with their saliva."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي مَعْشَرٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، أَنَّ الْحَسَنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ، رَأَى فِي قَمِيصِهِ دَمَا، فَبَزَقَ فِيهِ، ثُمَّ دَلَكَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū Ma‘shar → Yazīd ibn Abī Ziyād → Al-Ḥasan ibn ‘Alī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Abū Ma‘shar, from Yazīd ibn Abī Ziyād, that Al-Ḥasan ibn ‘Alī saw blood on his shirt, so he spat on it and then rubbed it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي سَلِيطُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، رَأَى فِي حِرْمَانِهِ دَمَا، فَبَزَقَ فِيهِ، ثُمَّ دَلَكَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥusayn ibn Ja‘far → Salīṭ ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn Yasār → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ḥusayn ibn Ja‘far, who said: Salīṭ ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn Yasār told me, saying: "I saw Ibn ‘Umar see blood on his Hirmān (garment), so he spat on it and then rubbed it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بُرْقَانَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مَيْمُونَ بْنَ مِهْرَانَ، يَوْمًا يُصَلِّي فَرَأَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ دَمًا، فَقَالَ بِهِ هَكَذَا، يَعْنِي، بِرِيقِهِ، ثُمَّ فَرَكَهُ بِيَدِهِ

english.narrator: Khālid ibn Ḥayyān → Ja‘far ibn Burqān → Maymūn ibn Mihrān
english.text: Khālid ibn Ḥayyān told us, from Ja‘far ibn Burqān, who said: "I saw Maymūn ibn Mihrān praying one day, and he saw blood on his garment. He did like this to it—meaning with his saliva—then rubbed it with his hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، وَعَامِرٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، قَالُوا: لَا يُغْسَلُ الدَّمُ بِالْبُزَاقِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Isrā’īl → Jābir → Abū Ja‘far + ‘Āmir + ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Isrā’īl, from Jābir, from Abū Ja‘far, ‘Āmir, and ‘Aṭā’, who said: "Blood is not washed with saliva."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ رَأَى فِي ثَوْبِهِ دَمًا فَغَسَلَهُ، فَبَقِيَ أَثَرُهُ أَسْوَدَ، وَدَعَا بِمِقَصٍّ فَقَصَّهُ فَقَرَضَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Numayr → ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn Numayr told us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he saw blood on his garment and washed it, but its black trace remained. So he called for scissors and cut it, snipping it out.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حُرَيْثٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا غَسَلْتَ الدَّمَ فَبَقِيَ أَثَرُهُ فَلَا يَضُرُّكَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥurayth → Ash-Sha‘bī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ḥurayth, from Ash-Sha‘bī, who said: "If you wash the blood and its trace remains, it does not harm you."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ دَلْهَمٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Al-Faḍl ibn Dalham → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Al-Faḍl ibn Dalham, from Al-Ḥasan, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ كَرِيمَةَ ابْنَةِ هَمَّامٍ، قَالَتْ: سَمِعْتُ عَائِشَةَ، وَسُئِلَتْ عَنْ دَمِ الْحَيْضِ يُصِيبُ الثَّوْبَ، فَقَالَتْ: اغْسِلِيهِ، فَقَالَتْ: غَسَلْتُهُ فَلَمْ يَذْهَبْ أَثَرُهُ، فَقَالَتْ: اغْسِلِيهِ فَإِنَّ الْمَاءَ طَهُورٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → ‘Alī ibn Mubārak → Karīmah bint Hammām → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from ‘Alī ibn Mubārak, from Karīmah bint Hammām, who said: I heard ‘Ā’ishah being asked about menstrual blood getting on a garment. She said: "Wash it." She said: "I washed it, but its trace did not go away." She said: "Wash it, for water is purification."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ غُشِيَ عَلَيْهِ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ، قَالَ: يَتَوَضَّأُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī → ‘Amr → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī told us, from ‘Amr, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who fainted while sitting. He said: "He performs ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَفَاقَ الْمُصَابُ تَوَضَّأَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Hushaym told us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "If the afflicted person (who fainted) recovers, he performs ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ أَبِي زَائِدَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ، قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ عَائِشَةَ، فَقُلْتُ: حَدِّثِينِي عَنْ مَرَضِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالَتْ: نَعَمْ، مَرِضَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَثَقُلَ، فَأُغْمِيَ عَلَيْهِ، فَأَفَاقَ، فَقَالَ: ضَعُوا لِي مَاءً فِي الْمِخْضَبِ؟، قَالَتْ: فَفَعَلْنَا، قَالَتْ: فَاغْتَسَلَ، فَذَهَبَ لِينُوءَ فَأُغْمِيَ عَلَيْهِ، ثُمَّ أَفَاقَ، فَقَالَ: ضَعُوا لِي مَاءً فِي الْمِخْضَبِ، فَاغْتَسَلَ

english.narrator: Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī → Zā’idah → Mūsā ibn Abī Zā’idah → ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Utbah → ‘Ā’ishah → Messenger of Allah ﷺ
english.text: Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī told us, from Zā’idah, from Mūsā ibn Abī Zā’idah, who said: ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Utbah told me, saying: I came to ‘Ā’ishah and said: "Tell me about the illness of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ." She said: Yes, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ became ill and heavy, and he fainted. When he recovered, he said: "Put water for me in the washbasin." She said: So we did. She said: He bathed, then tried to get up but fainted [again]. Then he recovered and said: "Put water for me in the washbasin," and he bathed.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْوَارِثِ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ بَهْدَلَةَ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ طَلْحَةَ، أَنَّ عُثْمَانَ، كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ فِي كُلِّ يَوْمٍ مَرَّةً

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd aṣ-Ṣamad ibn ‘Abd al-Wārith → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → ‘Āṣim ibn Bahdalah → Mūsā ibn Ṭalḥah → ‘Uthmān
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Abd aṣ-Ṣamad ibn ‘Abd al-Wārith told us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from ‘Āṣim ibn Bahdalah, from Mūsā ibn Ṭalḥah, that ‘Uthmān used to bathe once every day.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ الْجَزَّارِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: إِنِّي لَأَغْتَسِلُ فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْبَارِدَةِ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → Yaḥyā ibn al-Jazzār → ‘Alī
english.text: Ghundar told us, from Shu‘bah, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from Yaḥyā ibn al-Jazzār, from ‘Alī, who said: "I indeed bathe on a cold night."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ فِي كُلِّ يَوْمٍ مَرَّةً

english.narrator: Sulaymān ibn Ḥarb → Ḥammād ibn Zayd → Hishām → His Father (‘Urwah)
english.text: Sulaymān ibn Ḥarb told us, saying: Ḥammād ibn Zayd told us, from Hishām, from his father, that he used to bathe once every day.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ فِي كُلِّ يَوْمٍ مَرَّةً

english.narrator: Sulaymān ibn Ḥarb → Ḥammād ibn Zayd → Ibn ‘Awn → Muḥammad
english.text: Sulaymān ibn Ḥarb told us, saying: Ḥammād ibn Zayd told us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Muḥammad, that he used to bathe once every day.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ لِي عَلِيٌّ: إِنِّي لَأَغْتَسِلُ فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْبَارِدَةِ مِنْ غَيْرِ جَنَابَةٍ لِأَتَجَلَّدَ بِهِ وَأَتَطَهَّرَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥumayd → Al-A‘mash → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Salamah → ‘Alī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ḥumayd, from Al-A‘mash, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Salamah, who said: ‘Alī said to me: "I indeed bathe on a cold night without Janābah (major impurity) to invigorate myself and purify myself."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي صَخْرَةَ جَامِعِ بْنِ شَدَّادٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ حُمْرَانَ بْنَ أَبَانَ، مَوْلَى عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عَفَّانَ يَقُولُ: كُنْتُ أَضَعُ لِعُثْمَانَ، طَهُورَهُ، فَمَا أَتَى عَلَيْهِ يَوْمٌ إِلَّا وَهُوَ يُفِيضُ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ نُطْفَةً مِنْ مَاءٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mis‘ar → Abū Ṣakhrah Jāmi‘ ibn Shaddād → Ḥumrān ibn Abān → ‘Uthmān ibn ‘Affān
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Mis‘ar, from Abū Ṣakhrah Jāmi‘ ibn Shaddād, who said: I heard Ḥumrān ibn Abān, the freed slave of ‘Uthmān ibn ‘Affān, say: "I used to prepare purification water for ‘Uthmān, and no day passed him but that he would pour a quantity of water over himself."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، قَالَ: ذَهَبْتُ مَعَ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، إِلَى الْفُرَاتِ، فَدَخَلَهُ بِثَوْبٍ، أَوْ قَالَ: بِمَيْزَرٍ، وَقَالَ: إِنَّ لَهُ لَمَسَاكِنًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Sharīk → Abū Farwah → Ibn Abī Laylā
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Sharīk told us, from Abū Farwah, who said: I went with Ibn Abī Laylā to the Euphrates, and he entered it with a garment, or he said: with a waist wrapper (Izār), and said: "Indeed, it has inhabitants."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْمُحَارِبِيُّ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي مَنْ، رَأَى حُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ، دَخَلَ الْمَاءَ بِإِزَارٍ، وَقَالَ: إِنَّ لَهُ سَاكِنًا

english.narrator: Al-Muḥāribī → Layth → One who saw Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī
english.text: Al-Muḥāribī told us, from Layth, who said: Someone who saw Ḥusayn ibn ‘Alī informed me that he entered the water with a waist wrapper and said: "Indeed, it has an inhabitant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي مَنْ، رَأَى عُمَرَ، مُسْتَنْقِعًا فِي الْمَاءِ وَعَلَيْهِ قَمِيصٌ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ، فَدَعَا بِمِلْحَفَةٍ فَوْقَ الْقَمِيصِ

english.narrator: Ibn Fuḍayl → Ḥuṣayn → One who saw ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn Fuḍayl told us, from Ḥuṣayn, who said: Someone who saw ‘Umar told me that he was soaking in the water wearing a shirt, then he came out and called for a sheet over the shirt.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ سَعْدٍ الْجَارِي، وَكَانَ مَوْلَى عُمَرَ، قَالَ: أَتَانَا عُمَرُ، صَادِرًا عَنِ الْحَجِّ فِي نَفَرٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالَ: يَا سَعْدُ، أَبْغِنَا مِنَادِيلَ، فَأُتِيَ بِمَنَادِيلَ، فَقَالَ اغْتَسِلُوا فِيهِ، فَإِنَّهُ مُبَارَكٌ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bishr → Hushaym ibn Sa‘d → Zayd ibn Aslam → ‘Amr ibn Sa‘d al-Jārī → ‘Umar
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bishr told us, saying: Hushaym ibn Sa‘d told us, saying: Zayd ibn Aslam told us, from ‘Amr ibn Sa‘d al-Jārī—who was a freed slave of ‘Umar—who said: ‘Umar came to us returning from Hajj with a group of Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: "O Sa‘d, seek towels for us." So towels were brought. He said: "Bathe in it [the sea/water], for it is blessed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ حُدَيْرٍ، عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ هِلَالٍ، عَنْ كَثِيرٍ، مَوْلَى سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: مَنْ ذَبَحَ ذَبِيحَةً فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → ‘Imrān ibn Ḥudayr → ‘Īsā ibn Hilāl → Kathīr (Freed slave of Salamah)
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, from ‘Imrān ibn Ḥudayr, from ‘Īsā ibn Hilāl, from Kathīr, the freed slave of Salamah, who said: "Whoever slaughters a sacrificial animal should perform ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ رَبِيعٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَذْبَحُ الْبَعِيرَ وَالشَّاةَ، قَالَ: إِنْ أَصَابَهُ دَمٌ غَسَلَهُ وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ وُضُوءٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Rabī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Rabī‘, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who slaughters a camel or a sheep. He said: "If blood gets on him, he washes it, and there is no ablution upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُصْعَبُ بْنُ الْمِقْدَامِ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ الرَّجُلُ ثُمَّ ذَبَحَ شَاةً لَمْ يَقْطَعْ ذَلِكَ طَهُورَهُ، وَإِنْ أَصَابَهُ دَمٌ غَسَلَهُ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يُصِبْهُ دَمٌ فَلَا شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Muṣ‘ab ibn al-Miqdām → Zā’idah → Al-Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Muṣ‘ab ibn al-Miqdām told us, from Zā’idah, from Al-Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "If a man performs ablution then slaughters a sheep, that does not break his purification. If blood gets on him, he washes it, and if no blood gets on him, there is nothing upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْلٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، دَخَلَ الْخَلَاءَ وَعَلَيْهِ خُفَّاهُ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَتَوَضَّأَ، ثُمَّ مَسَحَ عَلَيْهِمَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Salamah ibn Kuhayl → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Sufyān told us, from Salamah ibn Kuhayl, who said: "I saw Ibrāhīm enter the privy while wearing his leather socks, then he came out and performed ablution, then wiped over them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ ذَرٍّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، قَالَ: دَعَوْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعِيَّ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ التَّيْمِيَّ، فَدَخَلَا الْخَلَاءَ فِي أَخْفَافِهِمَا، ثُمَّ خَرَجَا، وَتَوَضَّئَا وَمَسَحَا عَلَى خِفَافِهِمَا، ثُمَّ صَلَّيَا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → ‘Umar ibn Dharr → ‘Abd al-Malik ibn al-Ḥārith → Ibrāhīm an-Nakha‘ī + Ibrāhīm at-Taymī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: ‘Umar ibn Dharr told us, from ‘Abd al-Malik ibn al-Ḥārith, who said: "I invited Ibrāhīm an-Nakha‘ī and Ibrāhīm at-Taymī. They entered the privy in their leather socks, then came out, performed ablution, wiped over their leather socks, and then prayed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ لَمْ يُسَمِّهِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَالْحَكَمِ، أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا إِذَا أَرَادَا أَنْ يَبُولَا لَبِسَا خِفَافَهُمَا كَيْ يَمْسَحَاهَا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → A man he did not name → Ibrāhīm + Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Sufyān informed us, from a man he did not name, from Ibrāhīm and Al-Ḥakam, that when they wanted to urinate, they would put on their leather socks so that they could wipe over them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ حُدَيْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مِجْلَزٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الثَّوْبِ جَنَابَةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → ‘Imrān ibn Ḥudayr → Abū Mijlaz
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, from ‘Imrān ibn Ḥudayr, from Abū Mijlaz, who said: "There is no Janābah (major impurity) on the garment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، عَنْ أَبِي عَوَانَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: الثَّوْبُ لَا يُجْنِبُ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Ādam → Abū ‘Awānah → Abū Bishr → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Ādam told us, from Abū ‘Awānah, from Abū Bishr, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: "The garment does not become Junub."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ زَكَرِيَّا، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: الثَّوْبُ لَا يُجْنِبُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Zakariyyā → Ash-Sha‘bī → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Zakariyyā, from Ash-Sha‘bī, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: "The garment does not become Junub."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَتَوَضَّأُ فَيَجِفُّ وُضُوءُهُ، قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ فِي عَمَلِ الْوُضُوءِ غَسَلَ رِجْلَيْهِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ فِي غَيْرِ عَمَلِ الْوُضُوءِ اسْتَأْنَفَ الْوُضُوءَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ar-Rabī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, from Ar-Rabī‘, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who performs ablution and his ablution dries. He said: "If he is in the process of ablution, he washes his feet; and if he is doing something other than ablution, he restarts the ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ: يَغْسِلُ قَدَمَيْهِ، قُلْتُ: وَإِنْ جَفَّ وُضُوءُهُ، قَالَ: وَإِنْ جَفَّ الْوُضُوءُ قَالَ: وَكَذَلِكَ نَقُولُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: I asked Sufyān about that, and he said: "He washes his feet." I said: "Even if his ablution (previous parts washed) has dried?" He said: "Even if the ablution has dried." He [Wakī‘] said: "And this is what we say."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَعَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّهُمَا كَرِهَا أَنْ يَكْتُبَ الْجُنُبُ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Layth → Mujāhid + Jābir → Ash-Sha‘bī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Sufyān told us, from Layth, from Mujāhid, and from Jābir, from Ash-Sha‘bī, that they both disliked that a person in a state of major ritual impurity (Junub) writes 'In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful'.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا لَا يَرَوْنَ بَأْسًا أَنْ يَكْتُبَ الرَّجُلُ الرِّسَالَةَ وَهُوَ عَلَى غَيْرِ وُضُوءٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Sufyān told us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "They used to see no harm in a man writing a letter while he is without ablution."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي سِنَانٍ ضِرَارِ بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْهُذَيْلِ الْعَنْزِيِّ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ إِلَّا الْجَنَابَةِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Isrā’īl → Abū Sinān Ḍirār ibn Murrah → ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Hudhayl al-‘Anzī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Isrā’īl, from Abū Sinān Ḍirār ibn Murrah, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Hudhayl al-‘Anzī, who said: "They used to remember Allah in all states except in the state of Janābah (major ritual impurity)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، وَعَامِرٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، قَالُوا: لَيْسَ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنَ الشَّرَابِ وُضُوءٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Isrā’īl → Jābir → Abū Ja‘far + ‘Āmir + ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, from Isrā’īl, from Jābir, from Abū Ja‘far, ‘Āmir, and ‘Aṭā’, who said: "There is no ablution [required] from any drink."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيِّ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، أَنَّهُ سَقَاهُمْ مَرَّةً نَبِيذًا فَتَوَضَّأُوا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-Wahhāb ath-Thaqafī → Khālid → Abū Qilābah
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, from ‘Abd al-Wahhāb ath-Thaqafī, from Khālid, from Abū Qilābah, that he once gave them Nabīdh to drink, and they performed ablution.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الْأَقْطَعِ: إِذَا قُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ مِنَ الْمِفْصَلِ فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يَتَوَضَّأَ غَسَلَ الْقَطْعَ، وَإِذَا قُطِعَتِ الْكَفُّ غَسَلَ إِلَى الْمِرْفَقِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Sahl ibn Yūsuf → ‘Amr → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Sahl ibn Yūsuf told us, from ‘Amr, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding the amputee: "If his hand is cut off from the joint and he wants to perform ablution, he washes the stump. And if the palm is cut off, he washes up to the elbow."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّ زَيْدَ بْنَ ثَابِتٍ، أَصَابَهُ سَلَسٌ مِنْ بَوْلٍ، فَكَانَ يُصَلِّي وَهُوَ لَا يَرْقَأُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Yamān → Ma‘mar → Az-Zuhrī → Zayd ibn Thābit
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn Yamān told us, from Ma‘mar, from Az-Zuhrī, that Zayd ibn Thābit suffered from urinary incontinence, and he used to pray even though it did not stop.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَلْقَمَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: نُبِّئْتُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَانَتْ تُرَجِّلُهُ الْحَائِضُ، وَيَقُولُ: إِنَّ حَيْضَتَهَا لَيْسَتْ فِي يَدِهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Salamah ibn ‘Alqamah → Muḥammad
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Salamah ibn ‘Alqamah, from Muḥammad, who said: I was informed that the Prophet ﷺ used to have his hair combed by a menstruating woman, and he would say: "Her menstruation is not in her hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، وَيَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ تَمِيمِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كُنْتُ أُرَجِّلُ رَأْسَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ - - وَأَنَا حَائِضٌ وَهُوَ عَاكِفٌ

english.narrator: Ibn Numayr + Ya‘lā ibn ‘Ubayd → Al-A‘mash → Tamīm ibn Salamah → ‘Urwah → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Ibn Numayr and Ya‘lā ibn ‘Ubayd told us, from Al-A‘mash, from Tamīm ibn Salamah, from ‘Urwah, from ‘Ā’ishah, who said: "I used to comb the head of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while I was menstruating and he was in I‘tikāf (seclusion in the mosque)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: رُبَّمَا وَضَّأَتْهُ جَارِيَةٌ مِنْ جَوَارِيهِ وَهِيَ حَائِضٌ تَغْسِلُ قَدَمَيْهِ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah ibn Sulaymān → ‘Ubayd Allāh → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: ‘Abdah ibn Sulaymān told us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "Sometimes a slave girl from his slave girls would help him perform ablution while she was menstruating, washing his feet."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ جَارِيَةً كَانَتْ تَغْسِلُ رِجْلَيْهِ وَهِيَ حَائِضٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Sufyān told us, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār, from Ibn ‘Umar, that a slave girl used to wash his feet while she was menstruating.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُدْنِي رَأْسَهُ إِلَيَّ وَأَنَا حَائِضٌ وَهُوَ مُجَاوِرٌ، تَعْنِي مُعْتَكِفًا، فَيَضَعُهُ فِي حِجْرِي، فأَغْسِلُهُ وَأُرَجِّلُهُ وَأَنَا حَائِضٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Hishām → His Father (‘Urwah) → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Hishām told us, from his father, from ‘Ā’ishah, who said: "The Prophet ﷺ used to bring his head close to me while I was menstruating and he was Mujāwir—meaning in I‘tikāf—and place it in my lap, so I would wash it and comb it while I was menstruating."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، إِنَّ أَبَا ظَبْيَانَ سَأَلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْحَائِضِ تُوَضِّئُ الْمَرِيضَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Mughīrah → Abū Ẓabyān → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Mughīrah, that Abū Ẓabyān asked Ibrāhīm about a menstruating woman helping a sick person perform ablution. He said: "There is no harm in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ تَغْسِلَ الْحَائِضُ رَأْسَ الرَّجُلِ وَتُرَجِّلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ar-Rabī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ar-Rabī‘, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: "There is no harm if a menstruating woman washes a man's head and combs it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ مَنْبُوذٍ، عَنْ أُمِّهِ، قَالَتْ: دَخَلَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَلَى مَيْمُونَةَ، فَقَالَتْ: أَيْ بُنَيَّ، مَا لِي أَرَاكَ شَعْثًا رَأْسُكَ، قَالَ: إِنَّ أُمَّ عَمَّارٍ مُرَجِّلَتِي حَائِضٌ، قَالَتْ: أَيْ بُنَيَّ، وَأَيْنَ الْحَيْضَةُ مِنَ الْيَدِ، كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَضَعُ رَأْسَهُ فِي حِجْرِ إِحْدَانَا وَهِيَ حَائِضٌ

english.narrator: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah → Manbūdh → His Mother → Ibn ‘Abbās + Maymūnah
english.text: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah told us, from Manbūdh, from his mother, who said: Ibn ‘Abbās entered upon Maymūnah, and she said: "O my son, why do I see your head disheveled?" He said: "Umm ‘Ammār, my comber, is menstruating." She said: "O my son, where is menstruation from the hand? The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to place his head in the lap of one of us while she was menstruating."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نَا عُمَرُ بْنُ أَيُّوبَ الْمَوْصِلِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، فِي الْمَرِيضِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَتَوَضَّأَ، قَالَ: يَتَيَمَّمُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Umar ibn Ayyūb al-Mawṣilī → Ibn Abī Dhi’b → Az-Zuhrī
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Umar ibn Ayyūb al-Mawṣilī told us, from Ibn Abī Dhi’b, from Az-Zuhrī, regarding the sick person who cannot perform ablution. He said: "He performs Tayammum (dry ablution)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَا فِي الْمَرِيضِ تُصِيبُهُ الْجَنَابَةُ فَيَخَافُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ، قَالَ: هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْمُسَافِرِ الَّذِي لَا يَجِدُ الْمَاءَ، يَتَيَمَّمُ وَسَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً، فَقَالَ: لَا بُدَّ مِنَ الْمَاءِ وَيُسَخَّنُ لَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim → Qays ibn Sa‘d → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr + Mujāhid + ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Abū Usāmah told us, saying: Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim told us, from Qays ibn Sa‘d, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr and Mujāhid, who said regarding a sick person who becomes Junub and fears for himself. He said: "He is like the traveler who cannot find water; he performs Tayammum." And I asked ‘Aṭā’, and he said: "Water is necessary, and it should be heated for him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بَقِيُّ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: نا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، "أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ زَيْدٍ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ جَاءَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ , فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، رَأَيْتُ فِي الْمَنَامِ كَأَنَّ رَجُلًا قَامَ وَعَلَيْهِ بُرْدَانِ أَخْضَرَانِ عَلَى جِذْمَةِ حَائِطٍ، فَأَذَّنَ مَثْنَى، وَأَقَامَ مَثْنَى، وَقَعَدَ قَعْدَةً"، قَالَ: فَسَمِعَ ذَلِكَ بِلَالٌ، فَقَامَ، فَأَذَّنَ مَثْنَى، وَأَقَامَ مَثْنَى، وَقَعَدَ قَعْدَةً

english.narrator: Abū ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān Baqī ibn Makhlad → Abū Bakr ibn Abī Shaybah → Wakī‘ → Al-A‘mash → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā → Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ
english.text: Abū ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān Baqī ibn Makhlad told us, saying: Abū Bakr ibn Abī Shaybah told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, saying: Al-A‘mash told us, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, who said: The Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ told us that ‘Abd Allāh ibn Zayd al-Anṣārī came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I saw in a dream as if a man stood wearing two green garments on a stump of a wall, and he called the Adhan twice (phrases repeated twice), and called the Iqamah twice, and sat for a while." He said: "Bilāl heard that, so he stood up and called the Adhan twice, and called the Iqamah twice, and sat for a while."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: نا هَمَّامُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، عَنْ عَامِرٍ الْأَحْوَلِ، أَنَّ مَكْحُولًا، حَدَّثَهُ أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مُحَيْرِيزٍ، حَدَّثَهُ أَنَّ أَبَا مَحْذُورَةَ، حَدَّثَهُ قَالَ: "عَلَّمَنِي النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْأَذَانَ تِسْعَ عَشْرَةَ كَلِمَةً، وَالْإِقَامَةَ سَبْعَ عَشْرَةَ كَلِمَةً، الْأَذَانُ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ , اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ , اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، وَالْإِقَامَةُ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، قَدْ قَامَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، قَدْ قَامَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Affān → Hammām ibn Yaḥyā → ‘Āmir al-Aḥwal → Makḥūl → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Muḥayrīz → Abū Maḥdhūrah
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Affān told us, saying: Hammām ibn Yaḥyā told us, from ‘Āmir al-Aḥwal, that Makḥūl told him that ‘Abd Allāh ibn Muḥayrīz told him that Abū Maḥdhūrah told him, saying: "The Prophet ﷺ taught me the Adhan consisting of nineteen words, and the Iqamah consisting of seventeen words. The Adhan is: Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar; Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah, Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah; Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah, Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah; Hayya 'ala as-salah, Hayya 'ala as-salah; Hayya 'ala al-falah, Hayya 'ala al-falah; Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar; La ilaha illa Allah. And the Iqamah is: Allahu Akbar Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar Allahu Akbar; Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah, Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah; Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah, Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah; Hayya 'ala as-salah, Hayya 'ala as-salah; Hayya 'ala al-falah, Hayya 'ala al-falah; Qad qamat as-salah, Qad qamat as-salah; Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar; La ilaha illa Allah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَذَانُ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، شَهِدْتُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، شَهِدْتُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، شَهِدْتُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، ثَلَاثًا، شَهِدْتُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، شَهِدْتُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، شَهِدْتُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، ثَلَاثًا، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، ثَلَاثًا، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، ثَلَاثًا، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَحْسِبُهُ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Ayyūb, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "The Adhan of Ibn ‘Umar was: Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar; I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, I bear witness that there is no god but Allah—three times; I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah—three times; Hayya 'ala as-salah—three times; Hayya 'ala al-falah—three times; Allahu Akbar—I think he said La ilaha illa Allah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: "كَانَ الْأَذَانُ أَنْ يَقُولَ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn ‘Awn → Muḥammad
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Muḥammad, who said: "The Adhan was to say: Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar; Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah, Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah; Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah, Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah; Hayya 'ala as-salah, Hayya 'ala as-salah; Hayya 'ala al-falah, Hayya 'ala al-falah; Allahu Akbar Allahu Akbar; La ilaha illa Allah, and Allahu Akbar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْحَسَنُ، يَقُولُ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ فَيَقُولُ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، حَيَّ عَلَى الْفَلَاحِ، مَرَّتَيْنِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Yūnus, who said: Al-Ḥasan used to say: "Allahu Akbar; Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah; Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah; Hayya 'ala as-salah; Hayya 'ala al-falah." Then he would return and say: "Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar; Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah; Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah; Hayya 'ala as-salah; Hayya 'ala al-falah—twice; Allahu Akbar Allahu Akbar; La ilaha illa Allah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ التَّيْمِيُّ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مَحْذُورَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَخْفِضُ صَوْتَهُ بِالْأَذَانِ مَرَّةً مَرَّةً، حَتَّى إِذَا انْتَهَى إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ، رَجَعَ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، فَرَفَعَ بِهَا صَوْتَهُ مَرَّتَيْنِ مَرَّتَيْنِ، حَتَّى إِذَا انْتَهَى إِلَى حَيَّ عَلَى الصَّلَاةِ، قَالَ: الصَّلَاةُ خَيْرٌ مِنَ النَّوْمِ، فِي أَذَانِ الْأَوَّلِ فِي الْفَجْرِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Sulaymān at-Taymī → Ḥabīb ibn Qays → Ibn Abī Maḥdhūrah → His Father (Abū Maḥdhūrah)
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Yazīd ibn Hārūn told us, saying: Sulaymān at-Taymī informed us, from Ḥabīb ibn Qays, from Ibn Abī Maḥdhūrah, from his father, that he used to lower his voice in the Adhan once each, until when he reached the phrase 'Ashhadu anna Muhammadan Rasulullah', he returned to the phrase 'Ashhadu an la ilaha illa Allah' and raised his voice with it twice each. Until when he reached 'Hayya 'ala as-salah', he said: 'As-salatu khayrun min an-nawm' (Prayer is better than sleep), in the first Adhan of Fajr.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَصْحَابُنَا، أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ جَاءَ، فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنِّي لَمَّا رَجَعْتُ الْبَارِحَةَ وَرَأَيْتُ مِنِ اهْتِمَامِكَ، رَأَيْتُ كَأَنَّ رَجُلًا قَائِمًا عَلَى الْمَسْجِدِ عَلَيْهِ ثَوْبَانِ أَخْضَرَانِ، فَأَذَّنَ، ثُمَّ قَعَدَ قَعْدَةً، ثُمَّ قَامَ، فَقَالَ مِثْلَهَا، غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: قَدْ قَامَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، وَلَوْلَا أَنْ تَقُولُوا لَقُلْتُ: إِنِّي كُنْتُ يَقْظَانًا غَيْرَ نَائِمٍ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَقَدْ أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْرًا، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: أَمَا إِنِّي قَدْ رَأَيْتُ مِثْلَ الَّذِي رَأَى، غَيْرَ أَنِّي لَمَّا سُبِقْتُ اسْتَحْيَيْتُ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: مُرُوا بِلَالًا فَلْيُؤَذِّنْ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ghundar → Shu‘bah → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → Ibn Abī Laylā → Our companions
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ghundar told us, from Shu‘bah, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from Ibn Abī Laylā, who said: Our companions told us that a man from the Anṣār came and said: "O Messenger of Allah, when I returned last night and saw your concern, I saw as if a man standing on the mosque wearing two green garments called the Adhan, then sat for a while, then stood up and said the same, except that he added 'Qad qamat as-salah' (Prayer has commenced). And were it not that you would say [something], I would say that I was awake and not asleep." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Allah has shown you good." ‘Umar said: "Indeed, I saw similar to what he saw, but when I was preceded, I felt shy." The Prophet ﷺ said: "Order Bilāl to call the Adhan."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ، قَالَ: ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، بِنَحْوٍ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Fuḍayl → Ḥuṣayn → ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā → Prophet ﷺ
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn Fuḍayl told us, from Ḥuṣayn, from ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, from the Prophet ﷺ, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ رُفَيْعٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَحْذُورَةَ، أَنَّ أَذَانَهُ كَانَ مَثْنَى، وَأَنَّ إِقَامَتَهُ كَانَتْ وَاحِدَةً

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → ‘Abd al-‘Azīz ibn Rufay‘ → Abū Maḥdhūrah
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Jarīr told us, from ‘Abd al-‘Azīz ibn Rufay‘, from Abū Maḥdhūrah, that his Adhan was double (phrases repeated twice) and his Iqamah was single (phrases said once).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُثَنَّى، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: كَانَ بِلَالٌ يَشْفَعُ الْأَذَانَ، وَيُوتِرُ الْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Ḥajjāj → Abū al-Muthannā → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Mu‘āwiyah told us, from Ḥajjāj, from Abū al-Muthannā, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "Bilāl used to double the Adhan and make the Iqamah singular (odd)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، قَالَ: أَظُنُّهُ عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: أُمِرَ بِلَالٌ أَنْ يَشْفَعَ الْأَذَانَ، وَيُوتِرَ الْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ath-Thaqafī → Ayyūb → Abū Qilābah → Anas
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ath-Thaqafī told us, from Ayyūb, from Abū Qilābah, who said: I think he narrated from Anas, who said: "Bilāl was ordered to double the Adhan and make the Iqamah singular."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، أُمِرَ بِلَالٌ أَنْ يَشْفَعَ الْأَذَانَ، وَيُوتِرَ الْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Khālid → Abū Qilābah → Anas
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Khālid, from Abū Qilābah, from Anas: "Bilāl was ordered to double the Adhan and make the Iqamah singular."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: الْأَذَانُ مَثْنَى، وَالْإِقَامَةُ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abdah → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Anas
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Abdah told us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Anas, who said: "The Adhan is double, and the Iqamah is single."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، أَنَّ أَبَاهُ، كَانَ يَشْفَعُ الْأَذَانَ، وَيُوتِرَ الْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abdah → Hishām → ‘Urwah → His Father (Az-Zubayr ibn al-‘Awwām)
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Abdah told us, from Hishām, from ‘Urwah, that his father used to double the Adhan and make the Iqamah singular.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ التَّيْمِيِّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي رَجُلٌ، فِي مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: الْأَذَانُ مَثْنَى، وَالْإِقَامَةُ وَاحِدَةٌ، قَالَ: كَذَلِكَ أَذَانُ بِلَالٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Sulaymān at-Taymī → A man → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Sulaymān at-Taymī, who said: A man in the mosque of Kufa told me, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "The Adhan is double, and the Iqamah is single." He said: "Such was the Adhan of Bilāl."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: الْأَذَانُ مَثْنَى، وَالْإِقَامَةُ وَاحِدَةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abdah → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Anas
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Abdah told us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Anas, who said: "The Adhan is double, and the Iqamah is single."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَقُولُ: الْإِقَامَةُ مَرَّةً مَرَّةً، فَإِذَا قَالَ: قَدْ قَامَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، قَالَ: مَرَّتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: He used to say: "The Iqamah is once, once." But when he said: 'Qad qamat as-salah', he said: "Twice."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: أَقَمْتُ مَعَهُ بِدَابِقٍ، فَلَمْ يَكُنْ يَزِيدُ عَلَى إِقَامَةٍ، وَلَا يُؤَذِّنُ وَيَجْعَلُهَا وَاحِدَةً

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd → Makḥūl
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Abū Usāmah told us, from ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd, from Makḥūl, who said: "I stayed with him at Dābiq, and he would not increase beyond the Iqamah, nor would he call the Adhan, and he made it single."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُثَنَّى، أَنَّ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، كَانَ يَأْمُرُ الْمُؤَذِّنَ أَنْ يَشْفَعَ الْأَذَانَ، وَيُوتِرَ الْإِقَامَةَ، لِيَعْلَمَ الْمَارُّ الْأَذَانَ مِنَ الْإِقَامَةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abdah → Ismā‘īl → Abū al-Muthannā → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Abdah told us, from Ismā‘īl, from Abū al-Muthannā, that Ibn ‘Umar used to order the Muezzin to double the Adhan and make the Iqamah singular, so that the passerby would distinguish the Adhan from the Iqamah.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا، كَانَ يَقُولُ: الْأَذَانُ وَالْإِقَامَةُ مَثْنَى، وَأَتَى عَلَى مُؤَذِّنٍ يُقِيمُ مَرَّةً مَرَّةً، فَقَالَ: أَلَا جَعَلْتَهَا مَثْنَى لَا أُمَّ لِلْآخَرِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Hushaym ibn ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Yaḥyā → Ar-Rabī‘ ibn Qays → ‘Alī
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Hushaym ibn ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Yaḥyā told us, from Ar-Rabī‘ ibn Qays, that ‘Alī used to say: "The Adhan and the Iqamah are double." He came upon a Muezzin who was calling the Iqamah once, once, so he said: "Will you not make it double? May the other one have no mother (an expression of scolding)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدٍ، مَوْلَى سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ، أَنَّ سَلَمَةَ بْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ، كَانَ يُثَنِّي الْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ibrāhīm ibn Ismā‘īl → ‘Ubayd, the freed slave of Salamah ibn al-Akwa‘ → Salamah ibn al-Akwa‘
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Wakī‘ told us, from Ibrāhīm ibn Ismā‘īl, from ‘Ubayd, the freed slave of Salamah ibn al-Akwa‘, that Salamah ibn al-Akwa‘ used to double the Iqamah.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ زَيْدٍ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، مُؤَذِّنُ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَشْفَعُ الْأَذَانَ وَالْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Alī ibn Hāshim → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Zayd al-Anṣārī
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim told us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from ‘Abd ar-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, who said: "‘Abd Allāh ibn Zayd al-Anṣārī, the Muezzin of the Prophet ﷺ, used to double the Adhan and the Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، قَالَ: إِذَا جَعَلْتَهَا إِقَامَةً فَأَثْنِهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān → His Father → Shu‘ayb → Abū al-‘Āliyah
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulaymān told us, from his father, from Shu‘ayb, from Abū al-‘Āliyah, who said: "If you make it an Iqamah, then double it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا تَدَعُ أَنْ تُثَنِّيَ الْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Alī ibn Hāshim → Ibn Abī Laylā → Al-Ḥakam → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim told us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from Al-Ḥakam, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "Do not neglect doubling the Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: نا عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: ثنا الْحَجَّاجُ بْنُ أَرْطَاةَ، قَالَ: نا أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُ عَلِيٍّ، وَأَصْحَابُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَشْفَعُونَ الْأَذَانَ وَالْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Affān → ‘Abd al-Wāḥid ibn Ziyād → Al-Ḥajjāj ibn Arṭāh → Abū Isḥāq
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: ‘Affān told us, saying: ‘Abd al-Wāḥid ibn Ziyād told us, saying: Al-Ḥajjāj ibn Arṭāh told us, saying: Abū Isḥāq told us, saying: "The companions of ‘Alī and the companions of ‘Abd Allāh used to double the Adhan and the Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا أُسَامَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَعْشَرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِنَّ بِلَالًا، كَانَ يُثَنِّي الْأَذَانَ وَالْإِقَامَةَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Usāmah → Sa‘īd → Abū Ma‘shar → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Usāmah told us, from Sa‘īd, from Abū Ma‘shar, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "Bilāl used to double the Adhan and the Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: نا مُعَاوِيَةُ، عَنْ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، قَالَ: "كَانَ آخِرُ أَذَانِ بِلَالٍ: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Mu‘āwiyah → Al-A‘mash → Ibrāhīm → Al-Aswad
english.text: Abū Bakr told us, saying: Mu‘āwiyah told us, from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrāhīm, from Al-Aswad, who said: "The last part of Bilāl's Adhan was: La ilaha illa Allah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَا: "كَانَ آخِرُ آذَانِ بِلَالٍ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm + Ash-Sha‘bī
